text: Launch live hivemind test early in session while context is fresh — validate full worker lifecycle with stale fix applied
After Phase 1 gate, run Phase 2 prep: deliberate crash recovery test + multi-file worker tasks
Front-load 2+ tasks to worker at launch time — workers check inbox once then wrap after first task. Single-task worker sessions waste 50% of parallel capacity.
One task per message for workers — combined TASK 1+TASK 2 messages cause workers to pick the easiest and wrap. Send primary via launch_worker.sh, queue secondary via cca_comm.py task.
Worker wrap needs self-learning journal step — now added as Step 5. Workers log session outcomes + send advancement tips to desktop.
Start with MT work FIRST at session start — pick from priority queue immediately, do process improvements and daily scan AFTER first MT task
Launch worker and queue tasks BEFORE reading MASTER_TASKS.md in detail — worker needs max parallel time, and MASTER_TASKS eats 5K+ tokens of context budget
The desktop overload problem in 3-chat is solved by a structured COORD->WORK loop with a 2-min time budget, not by trying to multitask orchestration and coding simultaneously
Cross-project routing: cca_comm.py task km routes through cross_chat_queue.py — same interface, two backends. Simplest path to unified coordination.
